items.headline: Rockies put pitcher Ryan Feltner on the 15-day injured list with right ulnar nerve inflammation
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Colorado Rockies pitcher Ryan Feltner was placed on the 15-day injured list Friday with right ulnar nerve inflammation.
The team recalled left-hander Sammy Peralta from Triple-A Albuquerque before its series opener against the New York Mets.

items.headline: Cubs place left-handed reliever Caleb Thielbar on the 15-day injury list with left hamstring strain
items.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Chicago Cubs placed left-hander Caleb Thielbar on the 15-day injured list with a left hamstring strain on Friday.
The Cubs also transferred right-hander Porter Hodge to the 60-day injured list while infielder Scott Kingery was designated for assignment.

items.headline: Athletics hit 3 solo homer on 7 pitches off Rangers' Eovaldi in 1st inning
items.description:
ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — The Athletics got off to a fast start against the Texas Rangers on Friday night, hitting three solo home runs in the first inning.
Nick Kurtz, Carlos Cortes and Tyler Soderstrom — three of the first four hitters — homered just seven pitches into the game.

items.headline: Blue Jays OF Nathan Lukes leaves Friday's game in the 1st inning because of a sore left hamstring
items.description:
TORONTO (AP) — Toronto Blue Jays outfielder Nathan Lukes exited Friday’s game against Cleveland in the first inning because of a sore left hamstring.
Lukes doubled to right field to lead off the first against Cleveland’s Gavin Williams, but walked off the field following a brief discussion with the trainer and manager John Schneider.

items.headline: Mets say injured shortstop Francisco Lindor to be evaluated again in 3 weeks
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — The New York Mets provided some more information Friday on the status of injured shortstop Francisco Lindor.
There's still no projected timeline for Lindor's return, but the five-time All-Star will wear a protective boot on his lower left leg for the next week. He will undergo imaging again in three weeks and then be re-evaluated.

items.headline: Mariners trade right-hander Casey Legumina to Rays for minor league righty Ty Cummings
items.description:
SEATTLE (AP) — The Seattle Mariners traded right-hander Casey Legumina, who was designated for assignment on Tuesday, to the Tampa Bay Rays in exchange for minor-league right-hander Ty Cummings on Friday.
Legumina was designated for assignment to clear room on Seattle's roster for right-hander Alex Hoppe. Legumina, 28, appeared in eight games for Seattle this season. He went 0-1 with a 4.63 ERA, three walks and nine strikeouts. During Monday night’s 6-4 loss to the Athletics, Legumina gave up three runs and took the loss.

items.headline: Reds scratch designated hitter Eugenio Suárez from lineup vs Tigers due to back issue
items.description:
CINCINNATI (AP) — Cincinnati Reds designated hitter Eugenio Suárez was scratched from the lineup for Friday night's series opener with the Detroit Tigers due to a back issue.
The Reds did not release details on the nature or seriousness of the issue with Suárez.

items.headline: Yankees' left-hander Carlos Rodón scheduled for rehab start in return from left elbow surgery
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — New York Yankees pitcher Carlos Rodón is scheduled to make a rehab start with High-A Hudson Valley on Friday night.
Rodón opened the season on the 15-day injured list as he recovered from left elbow surgery last October. He suffered a setback in late March when he felt tightness in his right hamstring while throwing at the Yankees’ complex in Florida.

items.headline: Henderson, Ellis, Odom and Alderson to be inducted into Athletics' Hall of Fame
items.description:
Center fielder Dave Henderson, second baseman Mark Ellis, pitcher John “Blue Moon” Odom and executive Sandy Alderson will be inducted into the Athletics' Hall of Fame.
The inductions will take place on Saturday, Sept. 12, before the Athletics face Seattle.

items.headline: Braves CF Harris scratched from the lineup, then hits 2-run double as pinch-hitter vs Phillies
items.description:
ATLANTA (AP) — After being scratched from the Atlanta Braves lineup with an ailing left quad, Michael Harris II delivered as a pinch-hitter Friday night against the Philadelphia Phillies.
Harris drove a two-run double against the wall in left-center off Andrew Painter to give the Braves a 4-3 lead over the slumping Phillies, who came into the night with a nine-game losing streak.
